scope.summary: Implement partial-pattern completion path in author_garment: when the submitted GarmentSpec contains incomplete panels (panels with vertices_cm empty or fewer than 3 vertices) alongside a completion_hint, route to a diffusion-decoder backend that fills in the missing panel shapes and seams
acceptance_criteria: AuthorGarmentInput gains an optional completion_hint: Option<String> field that signals partial-pattern completion mode

When completion_hint is non-null and one or more panels in spec.panels have empty vertices_cm, TailorModelAdapter enters partial-completion mode: it constructs a CompletionRequest containing the partial GarmentSpec JSON and the completion hint, and prompts the LLM to fill in missing vertices_cm and seams
Fast validation is deferred for incomplete panels until after the completion pass; PANEL_CLOSURE is only checked on the completed result
The completion pass uses the same Tier-1 GarmentCodeRcDecoder factory infrastructure as the full-spec path (reuses the decoder from the ChatGarment Tier-1 MT)
The completed GarmentSpec is stored as a draft and the completion_hint is carried in GarmentSpec.natural_description
A completion_confidence: f32 field is added to SimulationReceipt so the model knows how much the completion diverged from the hint
ModelManual entry for author_garment documents the partial-completion mode, its preconditions, and when to prefer it over full-spec authoring
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::model_adapter::partial_completion_front_panel (GarmentSpec with one panel missing vertices + completion_hint produces a complete GarmentSpec passing fast validation)
cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::model_adapter::partial_completion_no_hint_rejected (partial panels without completion_hint return typed error InvalidSpec)
cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::mcp_tools::author_garment_partial_emits_draft_proposed (partial completion produces TailorGarmentDraftProposed event after completion)
scope.risk_if_missed: Operators and models must always supply a complete GarmentSpec with explicit panel vertices before simulation. This blocks the rapid-exploration workflow where a model is given a rough garment outline (e.g., front panel only or a garment type name with no vertex data) and completes the pattern automatically. Without partial-completion, the authoring loop requires significantly more model effort per iteration and cannot leverage diffusion-style completion models that are 100x faster than full-spec generation.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
